novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Misses Duarte et al. 2024, 'An Automated Approach to Causal Inference in Discrete Settings', a closer published generic finite-discrete sharp-bounds algorithmic comparator; without a strict separation from generic automated/LP sharp-bounds machinery, tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The bound G^eta_{P_rho}(D) >= c_fc/4 uses c_fc as a Hoffman-style feasibility margin normalized only through row/column constraints, but no scale map is defined from that margin to CDF-unit support gap G or through the envelope-renormalization step.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Duarte et al. 2024/JASA Autobounds as a comparator and state exactly why the endpoint-face complex is more than a special case of generic finite discrete sharp-bounds computation.

Replace Conjecture 2's c_fc/4 constant with a fully scaled perturbation theorem: define the RHS/constraint perturbation norm, the Hoffman or Farkas certificate normalization, and the Lipschitz conversion into CDF support-gap units.

To reach flagship, promote Conjecture 1 into a strict iff frontier against Firpo-Ridder/Russell/Duarte: identify a class where generic pointwise or generic LP reporting is provably insufficient and the certificate complex yields a new computable minimal obstruction.

recommended_next_step: Revise around a strict published-comparator frontier and repair the c_fc-to-G scaling before re-review.
